ROBERT J. LOGAN 
CITY ATTORNEY 
OFFICE OF 
CITY ATTORNEY 
CITY OF SAN JOSE 
151 WEST MISSION STREET 
SAN JOSE. CALIFORNIA 95110 
(408) 277·4454 
December 22, 1978 
State of california 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Re: 
Request for a Conflict of Interest Opinion 
Dear Gentlepersons: 
On behalf of the City Attorney's office of the City of 
San Jose, we request your opinion on the following situation: 
Councilmember James Self, sits as both a member of the 
City Council of the City of San Jose and as a Director of the 
Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Jose. 
Councilmember Self is aware of certain commercial property 
in the downtown of the City of San Jose which the owner desires 
to sell. This property is improved with commercial buildings in 
need of sorre rehabilitation. 
The property is situated approximately 
one block from the San Antonio redevelopment project area and approx-
imately and approximately two blocks fram the Pueblo Uno redevelopment 
project area. 
Councilmember Self is also aware of an interested purchaser for 
the subject co:mmercial property. 
Councilmember Self proposes to act as a "finder" in getting the 
interested seller and the interested purchaser together. If a sales 
transaction results, Councilmember Self will realize a finder's fee 
arrounting to several thousand dollars. 
He will have no ownership 
interest or other participation interest in the property. 
The only 
other service which might be provided will be to recommend to the 
buyer certain building contractors who would be available for rehabil-
itation of the buildings. 
There are no conditions attached to the 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
Re: 
Request for a Conflict of Interest Opinion 
Councilmernber Self 
Page 2 
12/22/78 
transaction which are related to the official duties of Council-
member Self, either as a Councilperson or as a Director of the 
Redevelopment Agency. 
Given the foregoing, the question arises: 
1. 
Will Councilmernber Self be required to disqualify 
himself from participating in decisions of the Redevelopment 
Agency or the City with respect to implementation of the 
San Antonio redevelopment project or the Pueblo Uno 
redevelopment project, assurrdng that the implementation 
of such plans will be to improve the ultimate fair market 
value of the commercial property at some time in the future. 
This question also assumes that none of the activities of 
the Redevelopment Agency or of the City will affect the fair 
market value of the property in the transaction between the 
interested buyer and the interested seller nor will the 
activities of the Agency or the City be involved with the 
amount of Councilmernber Self's finder's fee? 
2. 
Does it make a difference if the finder's fee 
accrues to Councilmember Self a) from the seller, b) from the 
buyer, or c) from a real estate agent who may later become 
involved in the transaction and the negotiations betwen 
buyer and seller? 
We appreciate your earliest response to this request, and if 
you have any request for further information, please do not hesitate 
to contact Deputy City Attorney Anthony C. Bennetti at (408) 277-
4458. 
ACB:lm 
cc : 
Councilrnember J. Self 
Very truly yours, 
ROBERI' J. lOGAN 
City Attorney

sections.question: 1. Will Councilmember Self be required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions of the Redevelopment Agency or the City with respect to implementation of the San Antonio redevelopment project or the Pueblo Uno redevelopment project, assuming that the implementation of such plans will be to improve the ultimate fair market value of the commercial property at some time in the future. This question also assumes that none of the activities of the Redevelopment Agency or of the City will affect the fair market value of the property in the transaction between the interested buyer and the interested seller nor will the activities of the Agency or the City be involved with the amount of Councilmember Self's finder's fee? 2. Does it make a difference if the finder's fee accrues to Councilmember Self a) from the seller, b) from the buyer, or c) from a real estate agent who may later become involved in the transaction and the negotiations between buyer and seller?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Councilmember who also serves as a Redevelopment Agency Director must recuse himself from participating in redevelopment decisions when he stands to earn a finder's fee from facilitating a commercial property sale near those project areas, and whether the source of the finder's fee affects this conflict analysis.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (not an FPPC response). Document dated 12/22/78, filed as 79-112. No FPPC conclusion or response is included in this document. Two specific questions are clearly articulated regarding conflict of interest and recusal requirements.

This question also assumes that none of the activities of the Redevelopment Agency or of the City will affect the fair market value of the property in the transaction between the interested buyer and the interested seller nor will the activities of the Agency or the City be involved with the amount of Councilmember Self's finder's fee? 2. embedding.summary: The City Attorney of San Jose requests an FPPC conflict of interest opinion regarding Councilmember James Self's proposed role as a 'finder' in a commercial property transaction while also serving as a City Council member and Redevelopment Agency Director, and whether he must recuse himself from redevelopment decisions that could indirectly benefit the property.
